name: card_number
description: Masked card number (PAN) used for the transaction. Always masked for PCI compliance — only the first six and last four digits are visible.
notes: Format varies by gateway. Some return 4111-11**-****-1111, others 411111******1111.

name: card_type
description: Type or brand of the card used for the transaction, such as credit, debit, or prepaid. Some gateways return the scheme name (Visa, Mastercard) instead.

name: card_issuer
description: The issuing bank or financial institution of the card. Useful for analytics and routing decisions.

name: card_holder
description: Full name of the cardholder as provided by the payment gateway.

name: cardholder_email
description: Email address of the cardholder, if provided by the payment gateway during the transaction.

name: card_expiry_month
description: Expiry month of the card, zero-padded to two digits.

name: card_expiry_year
description: Expiry year of the card. Format varies — some gateways return two digits, others four.
notes: May be "26" or "2026" depending on gateway.

name: full_card_expiry
description: Full card expiry date in MM/YY or MM/YYYY format, as a single combined string.

name: card_details
description: Additional card details object returned by the payment gateway. Contains gateway-specific card metadata not covered by other fields.
notes: Structure varies by gateway. Use the specific card_* fields for consistent access.

name: auth_code
description: Authorization code returned by the payment gateway for the transaction. This is the approval code from the issuing bank confirming the transaction was authorized.
notes: Always present for successful transactions. This is the only mandatory PG param.

name: transaction_id
description: Unique transaction identifier assigned by the payment gateway. Use this when communicating with the gateway about a specific transaction.

name: payment_id
description: Payment identifier from the payment gateway, used for tracking and reconciliation. May differ from transaction_id on gateways that separate payment and transaction concepts.

name: ref
description: Gateway-specific reference identifier for the transaction. Often used as the merchant's reference on the gateway side.

name: track_id
description: Tracking identifier assigned by the payment gateway for reconciliation purposes.

name: receipt_no
description: Receipt number generated by the payment gateway for the transaction. Useful for customer-facing receipts and records.

name: transaction_no
description: Transaction number assigned by the gateway. On some gateways this is different from transaction_id and serves as a secondary identifier.

name: rrn
description: Retrieval Reference Number — a banking-standard unique identifier used for transaction lookups, dispute resolution, and chargeback processing.
notes: Essential for disputes. Store this value and include it in any communication with the gateway or acquiring bank.

name: order_id
description: Order identifier as assigned by the payment gateway. Represents the gateway's internal order record.

name: result
description: Normalized transaction result from the payment gateway, indicating the outcome of the transaction.
notes: Common values: CAPTURED, APPROVED, DECLINED, ERROR. Values vary by gateway — use Ottu's top-level 'state' field for standardized status.

name: decision
description: The gateway's final decision on the transaction. Indicates whether the transaction was accepted, rejected, or flagged for review.
notes: Common values: ACCEPT, REJECT, REVIEW. Not all gateways provide this field.

name: pg_message
description: Human-readable message from the payment gateway describing the transaction outcome. Useful for logging and debugging.

name: post_date
description: The date the transaction was posted or settled by the payment gateway.

name: dcc_payer_amount
description: The amount in the payer's local currency when Dynamic Currency Conversion (DCC) is applied. This is the amount the cardholder actually pays.
notes: Only present when DCC is active. Compare with the transaction amount to see the conversion difference.

name: dcc_payer_currency
description: The payer's local currency code when DCC is applied, in ISO 4217 format.

name: dcc_payer_exchange_rate
description: The exchange rate applied for the Dynamic Currency Conversion between the merchant's currency and the payer's local currency.
notes: Multiply the transaction amount by this rate to get the payer amount.
